description:
Goal: remove the explicit legacy_fn_def compatibility seam introduced in fz-rh2.11.7.14 by porting contract/body/dispatch/keying to compiler2-owned quoted-source readers, leaving grouped quoted roots as the only function source surface and eliminating the last derived old-AST function carrier from compiler2.

Grounding:
- fz-rh2.11.7.14 made FunctionSource the authoritative function fact and legacy_fn_def the explicit temporary downstream artifact.
- jobs/contract.rs, jobs/body.rs, jobs/dispatch.rs, and related keying helpers still read def.legacy_ast today.
- the name is intentionally stinky so we can grep it later and remove it deliberately.

Signal:
- contract/body/dispatch/keying consume typed quoted-source readers or equivalent compiler2-owned function-surface records, not legacy_fn_def/FnDef
- FunctionDef no longer needs to carry legacy_ast as a stored compatibility field
- deleting src/compiler2/legacy_fn_def.rs is either part of this ticket or the ticket leaves a smaller final follow-up if one truly remains
- telemetry/tests prove the same source-root revision path still wakes downstream facts without any old-AST compatibility carrier

Change:
- add the typed quoted readers needed by downstream jobs
- port downstream users off def.legacy_ast
- remove legacy_fn_def storage and its now-dead helpers/tests/docs

Why:
- .14 made the temporary seam honest; this ticket is the cleanup price for that affordance
title: Compiler2 cleanup: remove legacy_fn_def compatibility seam
